rent.note: furnished 1BR or small apartment; centre can be lower, lakeside/nomad-ready units start nearer €300; summer spikes sharply

utilities.source: numbeo.com North Macedonia / citycost.org North Macedonia
utilities.note: electric + water + home internet; low A/C burden outside summer

groceries.note: home cooking with local markets; imported goods costlier

diningOut.note: local restaurants cheap; waterfront/tourist areas carry summer premium

transit.note: mostly walking plus occasional taxis/minibuses; no full metro-style pass

coworking.note: sparse formal coworking; budget assumes cafe working plus occasional paid workspace/day access

simData.note: local prepaid data plan; coverage around Ohrid is reasonable

misc.note: gym if available, toiletries, lake activities, entertainment, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Lakeside tourist town; extreme summer peak.

evidence.claim: 1BR centre can be low, but lakeside/nomad-ready rentals start near €300 and peak-season pricing is much higher
evidence.quote: In Ohrid's center, a one-bedroom apartment runs about $234 per month, with prices dropping to around $168 further out. Ohrid offers lakeside living from €300 per month

evidence.claim: digital nomad monthly budget can sit below €1,000 when cooking; Ohrid is cheaper than western European lake towns
evidence.quote: It would be very easy to live for under €1000 per month if cooking for yourself most nights

evidence.claim: North Macedonia supports a low all-in single-person budget, but Ohrid has a tourist-season premium
evidence.quote: a comfortable single-person budget starts at just €600–800 per month across North Macedonia

evidence.claim: Ohrid has sparse nomad infrastructure; cafes and apartment WiFi matter more than dedicated coworking
